rules.title: Oversized rounded corners
rules.codexPattern: Cards, buttons, and panels use 20–32px radius everywhere.
rules.recommendation: Reduce most radii to 8–12px. Use larger radius only for intentionally large containers.
rules.betterDirection: Use 8–10px for buttons and 8–12px for cards.

rules.title: Pill button and chip overload
rules.codexPattern: Most buttons and chips are fully rounded pills (border-radius 999px).
rules.recommendation: Use 8–10px radius for buttons. Reserve pills for a small number of genuine tag/filter chips.
rules.betterDirection: Solid or bordered buttons with consistent 8–10px radius, not pills.

rules.title: Unnecessary gradients
rules.codexPattern: Backgrounds, buttons, and text use soft corporate gradients to fake taste.
rules.recommendation: Replace decorative gradients with solid surfaces. Keep contrast and hierarchy doing the work.
rules.betterDirection: Flat, calm surfaces. Gradients only when they carry real meaning.

rules.title: Gradient text
rules.codexPattern: Headings, metrics, or CTA labels use gradient-filled text via `background-clip: text`.
rules.recommendation: Replace gradient text with a solid color. Use scale, weight, spacing, or one meaningful accent color for emphasis.
rules.betterDirection: Solid, legible text with hierarchy earned by typography rather than effects.

rules.title: Default cream / beige surface
rules.codexPattern: The main page background defaults to warm cream, sand, beige, parchment, or ivory.
rules.recommendation: Choose the surface from a concrete brand scene and color strategy. Use a true neutral, brand-tinted neutral, saturated brand field, or darker surface only when the product context supports it.
rules.betterDirection: A committed surface palette chosen from brand context, not default warm paper.

rules.title: Glow-heavy UI
rules.codexPattern: Cards and buttons use colored glow shadows instead of hierarchy.
rules.recommendation: Remove glows. Use subtle neutral shadows (max 0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.1)) and spacing for hierarchy.
rules.betterDirection: Replace glow with simple borders, spacing, and typography hierarchy.

rules.title: Glassmorphism as the default
rules.codexPattern: Floating frosted-glass panels with backdrop blur are the default visual language.
rules.recommendation: Use solid panel backgrounds with subtle borders. Drop the blur unless there is a real overlay reason.
rules.betterDirection: Solid surfaces with 1px borders, no glass effect.

rules.title: Excessive / dramatic shadows
rules.codexPattern: Large dramatic drop shadows (e.g. 0 24px 60px rgba(0,0,0,0.35)) on everything.
rules.recommendation: Cap shadows at a subtle 0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.1). Avoid floating-everything elevation.
rules.betterDirection: Subtle, consistent shadows; let borders and spacing define edges.

rules.title: Hairline border plus wide shadow
rules.codexPattern: Cards and buttons combine a 1px border with a wide, diffuse shadow blur.
rules.recommendation: Commit to one edge treatment: a clear border or a subtle shadow. If using shadow, keep blur tight and intentional.
rules.betterDirection: One defined edge language instead of border and decorative elevation stacked together.

rules.title: Decorative eyebrow labels
rules.codexPattern: Small <small>/eyebrow labels above headings ('TEAM COMMAND', 'MARCH SNAPSHOT').
rules.recommendation: Remove eyebrow labels. Lead with a clear heading and let hierarchy come from size and weight.
rules.betterDirection: Just h1/h2 with proper hierarchy, no decorative eyebrow.

rules.title: Uppercase label overuse
rules.codexPattern: Many muted labels use uppercase + letter-spacing.
rules.recommendation: Use normal sentence-case labels. Reserve uppercase for rare, intentional emphasis.
rules.betterDirection: Readable sentence-case labels with clear hierarchy.

rules.title: Fake premium / generic SaaS copy
rules.codexPattern: Decorative headers like 'Operational clarity without the clutter'.
rules.recommendation: Cut decorative copy. Use plain, specific labels that describe the actual content.
rules.betterDirection: Honest, functional copy from the product voice.

rules.title: Formulaic AI copy patterns
rules.codexPattern: Visible UI copy uses throat-clearing openers, rhetorical setup, binary contrast, or generic business phrasing.
rules.recommendation: Replace formulaic phrasing with direct, product-specific copy. State the value without setup, fake drama, or meta-commentary.
rules.betterDirection: Plain product copy: specific noun, specific action, concrete outcome.

rules.title: AI-slop phrase tells
rules.codexPattern: Visible copy uses common generated phrases, generic sentence openers, or a cluster of high-probability AI words.
rules.recommendation: Replace AI-slop phrasing with concrete product language: specific nouns, specific actions, and real context.
rules.betterDirection: Direct, grounded copy that sounds like this product and this team.

rules.title: AI punctuation tells
rules.codexPattern: Copy overuses em dashes, exclamation marks, or ellipses as a generated-writing rhythm.
rules.recommendation: Reduce punctuation theatrics. Use commas, periods, or sentence structure, and let the words carry emphasis.
rules.betterDirection: Cleaner punctuation with a more natural, less generated rhythm.

rules.title: Default one-font template stack
rules.codexPattern: The page relies on one familiar generated-UI font stack, usually Inter/system/Roboto, without a distinct display/body role.
rules.recommendation: Define a deliberate type system: keep the body font if it works, but add a distinct display/wordmark role or stronger scale where the brand needs it.
rules.betterDirection: Typography has named roles and hierarchy instead of one default stack everywhere.

rules.title: Emoji used as UI icons
rules.codexPattern: Feature cards, headings, buttons, or lists use emoji as decorative icons.
rules.recommendation: Replace decorative emoji with a real icon set, product-specific visuals, or plain text. Keep emoji only when it is part of the brand voice.
rules.betterDirection: Consistent icon system or no icon; content and hierarchy carry the section.

rules.title: Duplicate CTA intent
rules.codexPattern: The same user action appears under several different CTA labels.
rules.recommendation: Pick one label per CTA intent and reuse it consistently across nav, section actions, and footer.
rules.betterDirection: One clear action vocabulary: one intent, one label, one visual priority.

rules.title: Decorative section-number labels
rules.codexPattern: Eyebrows use labels like 'SECTION 01', 'QUESTION 05', or '00 / INDEX'.
rules.recommendation: Remove section-number/meta labels. Use a plain topic heading, or let the headline carry the section by itself.
rules.betterDirection: Specific section titles instead of decorative numbering.

rules.title: Over-centered default composition
rules.codexPattern: The block relies on centered text, centered containers, and symmetrical stacking by default.
rules.recommendation: Break the default symmetry: left-align the section header, use a split layout, or create one asymmetric focal area.
rules.betterDirection: Intentional alignment and asymmetry driven by the content, not automatic centering.

rules.title: Mobile viewport height risk
rules.codexPattern: Sections use `h-screen`, `min-h-screen`, or `height: 100vh` for full-height layouts.
rules.recommendation: Use responsive `min-height` with `100dvh`/safe-area handling and verify that key content is visible on mobile.
rules.betterDirection: Stable viewport sizing that works with mobile browser UI.

rules.title: Pure black surface
rules.codexPattern: Dark sections use pure `#000`, `rgb(0,0,0)`, or `bg-black` as the main surface.
rules.recommendation: Replace pure black surfaces with off-black or tinted dark neutrals such as charcoal, zinc, or brand-tinted near-black.
rules.betterDirection: Dark surfaces are calibrated, not default black.

rules.title: Placeholder / dead links
rules.codexPattern: Links or CTAs point to `#`, `javascript:void(0)`, or other placeholder destinations.
rules.recommendation: Connect links to real destinations. If a destination is not ready, disable the control visually and explain the unavailable state.
rules.betterDirection: Every visible action either works or is clearly unavailable.

rules.title: Image alt text is missing or generic
rules.codexPattern: Meaningful images have no `alt`, empty `alt`, or generic labels like `alt=\
rules.recommendation: Add useful alt text for meaningful images. Use empty alt only for truly decorative images.
rules.betterDirection: Images have intentional accessible descriptions or are explicitly decorative.

rules.title: Arbitrary z-index values
rules.codexPattern: The UI uses `z-index: 9999`, `z-[9999]`, or other arbitrary layering values.
rules.recommendation: Replace arbitrary z-index values with a small layering scale for nav, popover, modal, toast, and overlay.
rules.betterDirection: Layering is systematic and documented, not a pile of emergency values.

rules.title: Overlay can be clipped by overflow container
rules.codexPattern: Dropdowns, tooltips, menus, popovers, or modals are positioned inside an `overflow: hidden/auto/clip` container.
rules.recommendation: Render overlays in a portal, use `position: fixed`, native popover/dialog primitives, or let the containing overflow be visible.
rules.betterDirection: Interactive layers escape clipping containers and follow a deliberate z-index scale.

rules.title: Paragraph measure is too wide
rules.codexPattern: Long body copy spans a very wide container instead of being constrained to a readable measure.
rules.recommendation: Limit long paragraphs to roughly 60-70 characters per line with `max-width: 65ch` or an equivalent layout constraint.
rules.betterDirection: Readable text measure with clear heading/body rhythm.

rules.title: Hero block inside a dashboard
rules.codexPattern: A full hero strip with decorative copy sits inside an internal/app UI.
rules.recommendation: Remove the hero. Start internal UIs with the actual working content and a simple header.
rules.betterDirection: Standard app header, then real content — no hero inside dashboards.

rules.title: Hero lacks a clear headline
rules.codexPattern: The first viewport has no readable, concise headline that explains the product or offer.
rules.recommendation: Lead with one concise, specific headline that communicates the product value in under two seconds.
rules.betterDirection: A clear H1/value proposition first, then supporting copy and CTA.

rules.title: Hero supporting copy is too long
rules.codexPattern: The hero uses a long paragraph or multiple text blocks before the primary action.
rules.recommendation: Compress hero supporting copy to one short paragraph, ideally under 25 words.
rules.betterDirection: Short value clarification that points toward the CTA.

rules.title: Hero has no primary CTA
rules.codexPattern: The hero introduces the product but does not provide a clear next action above the fold.
rules.recommendation: Add one obvious primary CTA aligned with the most predictable user action.
rules.betterDirection: One clear primary action above the fold, with optional subtle secondary action.

rules.title: Hero has competing CTAs
rules.codexPattern: The hero presents several actions with similar visual weight.
rules.recommendation: Keep one dominant primary CTA and at most one quieter secondary CTA.
rules.betterDirection: Primary CTA is visually strongest; secondary action is text/link or low-emphasis.

rules.title: Tiny touch targets
rules.codexPattern: Buttons, links, or icon controls are smaller than a comfortable 44x44px tap area.
rules.recommendation: Increase interactive controls to at least 44x44px, or wrap small icons in a larger hit area with adequate spacing.
rules.betterDirection: Comfortable tap targets with visible spacing between adjacent actions.

rules.title: Icon-only button lacks accessible name
rules.codexPattern: An icon-only button or link has no visible text, aria-label, title, or labelled-by relationship.
rules.recommendation: Add a concise accessible name with `aria-label`, visible text, or an associated label for every icon-only control.
rules.betterDirection: Every control has a clear visual purpose and a programmatic accessible name.

rules.title: Weak primary action
rules.codexPattern: The block has no dominant action, or several actions have the same visual weight.
rules.recommendation: Make one action visually primary and move competing actions to secondary/link treatment.
rules.betterDirection: One clear primary button near the decision point; secondary actions are quieter.

rules.title: Hero visual is missing or not product-relevant
rules.codexPattern: The hero has no meaningful product visual, or the visual appears decorative and disconnected.
rules.recommendation: Use a relevant product screenshot, demo, real image, proof panel, or remove the visual if it adds no meaning.
rules.betterDirection: Visual proof that reinforces the headline and clarifies the offer.

rules.title: Re-drawn UI chrome
rules.codexPattern: Product previews are wrapped in fake browser bars, phone frames, code windows, traffic-light dots, or IDE chrome.
rules.recommendation: Remove fake chrome. Show the real product view, a real screenshot with a simple hairline border, or a plain content panel.
rules.betterDirection: Product content carries the preview; browser/device chrome is real or absent.

rules.title: Decorative repeating stripes
rules.codexPattern: Section or body backgrounds use repeating linear gradients as decorative striping.
rules.recommendation: Remove the stripes unless the surface is a real chart, measurement, print, map, or branded texture. Use content structure, imagery, or a plain surface instead.
rules.betterDirection: Surface treatment has a product or brand reason; no decorative filler stripes.

rules.title: Decorative grid-line background
rules.codexPattern: A two-axis CSS grid is drawn with linear-gradient hairlines behind ordinary content.
rules.recommendation: Reserve grid backgrounds for actual canvases, maps, blueprints, editors, or measurement tools. Otherwise use product structure or a plain surface.
rules.betterDirection: Grid visuals appear only when the UI is genuinely spatial, technical, or canvas-based.

rules.title: Hero lacks trust support
rules.codexPattern: The hero asks for a conversion without nearby proof, reassurance, or credibility signal.
rules.recommendation: Add concise trust support near the CTA only when real proof exists: logos, metrics, testimonial, or reassurance.
rules.betterDirection: Small proof or reassurance that supports the CTA without cluttering the hero.

rules.title: Hero scan flow is unclear
rules.codexPattern: Hero elements do not form a clear headline → copy → CTA → proof/visual reading path.
rules.recommendation: Reorder and align the hero around a clear scan path: headline, supporting copy, primary CTA, then proof/visual.
rules.betterDirection: One obvious visual path from value proposition to action.

rules.title: Hero visual may be performance-heavy
rules.codexPattern: The first viewport depends on a heavy background/video/animation before core content is useful.
rules.recommendation: Prioritize immediate headline, supporting copy, and CTA; keep video/animation optimized and non-blocking.
rules.betterDirection: Core hero content loads first; media supports rather than blocks comprehension.

rules.title: Metric/KPI card grid as default
rules.codexPattern: A 3–4 column grid of KPI metric cards is the first dashboard instinct.
rules.recommendation: Only use metric tiles when the numbers matter. Prioritize the primary metric and de-emphasize the rest.
rules.betterDirection: Lead with the metric that matters; avoid a default uniform KPI grid.

rules.title: Fake charts / metrics
rules.codexPattern: Donut charts and canvas graphs that exist only to fill space.
rules.recommendation: Remove charts that carry no real data. Show real numbers or nothing.
rules.betterDirection: Only chart real, product-specific data.

rules.title: Placeholder proof or stock metrics
rules.codexPattern: Proof areas use placeholder names, lorem ipsum, Acme/Jane Doe examples, or stock metrics like '10x faster' and 'trusted by 50,000+ teams'.
rules.recommendation: Use only verified proof. Replace stock names/metrics with real data, mark metrics as pending, or remove the proof slot.
rules.betterDirection: Credibility content is factual, sourced, or intentionally left blank.

rules.title: Placeholder-only form fields
rules.codexPattern: Inputs rely on placeholder text instead of visible or programmatic labels.
rules.recommendation: Give every field a visible label or a clear programmatic label. Keep placeholders only as examples, not as the field name.
rules.betterDirection: Labeled inputs with visible error, focus, loading, and success states.

rules.title: Left-border accent cards
rules.codexPattern: Repeated cards use a colored left border as the main visual accent.
rules.recommendation: Remove the decorative accent strip. Use hierarchy, grouping, background contrast, or one intentionally featured card instead.
rules.betterDirection: Cards have purposeful hierarchy rather than repeated colored rails.

rules.title: Random blue/cyan AI-dashboard colors
rules.codexPattern: 'Premium dark mode' = blue-black gradients plus cyan accents.
rules.recommendation: Use the project's real palette or a calm, dark muted scheme. Avoid defaulting to blue/cyan.
rules.betterDirection: Calm, dark muted colors from the project palette, not generic blue/cyan.

rules.title: Gray text on colored background
rules.codexPattern: Muted gray text sits on a colored, tinted, or saturated surface.
rules.recommendation: Use a darker shade of the background hue, a calibrated ink color, or white/near-white with enough contrast.
rules.betterDirection: Secondary text is color-aware and readable, not generic gray.

rules.title: Overpadded layout
rules.codexPattern: Sections and cards use very large padding (48px+) to look airy/expensive.
rules.recommendation: Use a consistent 16/24/32px spacing scale. Reserve large padding for genuinely large containers.
rules.betterDirection: Standard section padding (20–30px); no dead space for effect.

rules.title: Text-heavy block
rules.codexPattern: Large paragraphs or repeated explanatory copy carry the section instead of scannable structure.
rules.recommendation: Reduce copy density by grouping text into a clear heading, short body, bullets, or cards with visible hierarchy.
rules.betterDirection: Scannable structure: concise heading, short support, grouped details.

rules.title: Weak visual hierarchy
rules.codexPattern: Headings, labels, and body text are nearly the same size/weight.
rules.recommendation: Establish a clear type scale. Make the primary element noticeably larger/heavier.
rules.betterDirection: Strong h1 → h2 → body contrast; one clear focal point.

rules.title: Monotonous section rhythm
rules.codexPattern: Everything has the same size, density, and emphasis, so the section has no focal point.
rules.recommendation: Introduce a clear focal point: make the most important item larger or more prominent and group supporting content around it.
rules.betterDirection: One dominant element, supporting groups, and intentional spacing rhythm.

rules.title: Repetitive equal cards
rules.codexPattern: Three or more cards share identical size and visual weight.
rules.recommendation: Create hierarchy: feature one card and reduce the emphasis of supporting cards.
rules.betterDirection: Make one card visually dominant; vary size or emphasis.

rules.title: Default icon-tile feature cards
rules.codexPattern: A feature section uses three or more equal cards with a rounded icon tile above a heading and short body copy.
rules.recommendation: Break the feature-card template: make one feature dominant, move icons inline, group features by workflow, or switch to a bento/stacked structure.
rules.betterDirection: Feature hierarchy follows product value or workflow, not a cloned card pattern.

rules.title: Pricing plans lack decision hierarchy
rules.codexPattern: Pricing cards are presented as identical columns with no recommended plan or comparison rhythm.
rules.recommendation: Make one recommended plan visually dominant, keep secondary plans quieter, and align price, benefits, and CTA rows.
rules.betterDirection: Pricing comparison with one clear recommended option and fast horizontal scanning.

rules.title: Too many nested panels
rules.codexPattern: Multiple nested panel types (panel, panel-2, rail-panel, table-panel).
rules.recommendation: Flatten the structure. Use spacing and a single level of containers instead of nested shells.
rules.betterDirection: One level of panels; separate content with spacing, not nested boxes.

rules.title: Decorative badges
rules.codexPattern: Badges/tags on every row and nav item that do not communicate state.
rules.recommendation: Keep badges only where they convey real, changing state. Remove decorative ones.
rules.betterDirection: Functional badges only; plain text otherwise.

rules.title: Decorative announcement bubble
rules.codexPattern: A large pastel pill/bubble with a decorative dot and short availability copy like 'Coming soon' or 'Soon in Astana'.
rules.recommendation: Remove the oversized announcement bubble. Use plain text, a compact functional badge, or a real status row only if the availability/location state matters.
rules.betterDirection: Short availability copy set in normal hierarchy; no decorative dot or inflated pastel pill.

rules.title: Decorative hover transforms
rules.codexPattern: Hover effects translate/scale elements (e.g. translateX(2px) on nav links).
rules.recommendation: Use simple opacity/color hover changes (100–200ms). Avoid transform animations.
rules.betterDirection: Subtle color/opacity hover states, no transforms.

rules.title: Unbounded or sluggish UI motion
rules.codexPattern: UI transitions use `transition: all`, `ease-in`, or long durations on ordinary interface elements.
rules.recommendation: Specify exact animated properties, use responsive ease-out/custom curves, and keep routine UI motion under 300ms.
rules.betterDirection: Purposeful 125-250ms transitions on transform/opacity/color only.

rules.title: Bounce or elastic UI motion
rules.codexPattern: Routine UI motion uses bounce, elastic, wobble, jiggle, or springy named animations/easing.
rules.recommendation: Use smooth ease-out-quart/quint/expo style curves for UI motion. Reserve playful bounce only for rare, brand-appropriate moments.
rules.betterDirection: Confident deceleration, not novelty bounce.

rules.title: Scale-zero entrance
rules.codexPattern: Elements enter from `scale(0)` or Tailwind `scale-0` before becoming visible.
rules.recommendation: Start entrances from `scale(0.9–0.97)` plus opacity instead of `scale(0)`. Keep the movement subtle and quick.
rules.betterDirection: Physical entrance: small scale offset, opacity, and responsive ease-out timing.

rules.title: Trigger-anchored overlay uses center origin
rules.codexPattern: Popovers, dropdowns, menus, or tooltips scale from `transform-origin: center` instead of their trigger.
rules.recommendation: Use the trigger-aware transform origin from the component library, such as Radix `--radix-popover-content-transform-origin`, or an equivalent top/bottom/left/right origin. Keep modals centered.
rules.betterDirection: Anchored surfaces scale from the element that opened them; modals remain centered.

rules.title: Non-interruptible keyframes on rapid UI
rules.codexPattern: Toasts, toggles, popovers, accordions, or similar rapidly-triggered components use keyframe animations that restart from zero.
rules.recommendation: Prefer interruptible CSS transitions, `@starting-style`, WAAPI, or springs that retarget from the current state.
rules.betterDirection: Rapid UI motion can reverse or retarget smoothly instead of replaying a canned keyframe.

rules.title: Hover motion is not pointer-gated
rules.codexPattern: Hover transform/scale/translate motion is defined globally without a fine-pointer hover media query.
rules.recommendation: Gate hover motion behind `@media (hover: hover) and (pointer: fine)`, and keep touch feedback explicit.
rules.betterDirection: Hover motion only runs on devices with real hover; touch gets stable tap states.

rules.title: Layout-property animation
rules.codexPattern: Animations or transitions target width, height, margin, padding, top, left, right, or bottom.
rules.recommendation: Move motion to transform and opacity. Use translate/scale/clip-path for movement and reveal instead of layout properties.
rules.betterDirection: GPU-friendly transform/opacity motion with stable layout.

rules.title: Motion lacks reduced-motion fallback
rules.codexPattern: Transform/position animation is present without a `prefers-reduced-motion` fallback.
rules.recommendation: Add a reduced-motion path: keep helpful opacity/color transitions, but remove or shorten transform/position movement.
rules.betterDirection: Motion respects `prefers-reduced-motion` and keeps the interface understandable.

rules.title: Inconsistent / poor spacing
rules.codexPattern: Random gaps and padding values that do not follow a scale.
rules.recommendation: Adopt a consistent 4/8/12/16/24/32px spacing scale across the section.
rules.betterDirection: Consistent spacing scale; predictable rhythm.

rules.title: Floating sidebar shell
rules.codexPattern: A detached sidebar with rounded outer corners, margin, and shadow.
rules.recommendation: Use a normal fixed sidebar (240–260px, solid background, simple border-right, square outer corners).
rules.betterDirection: Solid edge-to-edge sidebar with a border-right, not a floating card.

rules.title: Generic AI navigation/footer template
rules.codexPattern: Navigation uses the default 'Features / Pricing / Docs / Blog / About' row, or footer uses Product / Company / Resources / Legal columns without real IA.
rules.recommendation: Rebuild navigation/footer around the actual information architecture. Keep only real destinations and choose a structure that matches the product type.
rules.betterDirection: Navigation/footer labels and grouping reflect this product, not a stock SaaS sitemap.

rules.title: Decorative status dots
rules.codexPattern: Colored ::before dots / 'live pulse' indicators used decoratively.
rules.recommendation: Use status indicators only for real status. Drop decorative pulses.
rules.betterDirection: Plain text or one functional indicator; no decorative dots.

rules.title: Too many muted labels
rules.codexPattern: Overuse of muted gray-blue text that weakens contrast and clarity.
rules.recommendation: Limit muted text. Keep primary content at full contrast and readable.
rules.betterDirection: Strong contrast for content; muted only for true secondary info.

rules.title: Random glass panels
rules.codexPattern: Frosted/blur-haze panels scattered as decoration with no overlay purpose.
rules.recommendation: Remove decorative glass panels. Use solid surfaces and structure instead.
rules.betterDirection: Solid, honest panels; no decorative glass.
